summary: Extract the three byte-identical *UpdateAkSk C-API wrapper bodies (kv/object/stream) into one header-only function template UpdateAkSkImpl<ClientT> in src/datasystem/c_api/util.h, dropping the redundant accessKey.assign no-op. Each wrapper becomes a single delegating return: kv and object instantiate with ObjectClientImpl (verified: the KV handle is uniformly ObjectClientImpl-backed), stream with StreamClient. Signatures, linkage, and error/success return semantics are preserved exactly; no BUILD/CMake edits.

ledger.what: Place the shared logic as a template<typename ClientT> free function in src/datasystem/c_api/util.h (header-only), rather than a non-template void* helper in util.cpp.
ledger.why: The three sites differ only by client type; the member call (*client)->UpdateAkSk(...) needs the concrete type, which a void* non-template cannot supply without a second dispatch. A header-only template matches the issue's 'templated/shared helper' request and needs no BUILD edits.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose the template-in-util.h approach; util.h already hosts sibling helpers ConnectWorker/FreeClient and is included by all three wrapper .cpp files.

blast_radius.why: Only the three wrapper .cpp files instantiate it; no other caller and no C-ABI change.

reversibility.why: It is an internal helper; inlining it back into the three sites is mechanical.

ledger.revisit_if: A reviewer prefers explicit instantiation in util.cpp, or a non-template void* dispatch is mandated for header-size reasons.

ledger.what: Drop the redundant accessKey.assign(cAccessKey, cAccessKeyLen) line entirely rather than preserving it in the helper.
ledger.why: The line reassigns the exact same bytes into a std::string just constructed from them (a pure no-op); the issue explicitly flags it as the bug and notes the fix must apply to all three sites, which extraction achieves.
ledger.what_i_did: Excluded the .assign call from the extracted helper so a single construction remains.

blast_radius.why: Removing a self-assignment of identical bytes cannot change accessKey's value or length.

reversibility.why: Re-adding one line if ever wanted is trivial.

ledger.revisit_if: Review insists on a literal no-behavior-change extraction that keeps the dead line.

ledger.what: The KV wrapper's SCUpdateAkSk casting the handle to datasystem::object_cache::ObjectClientImpl is intentional and correct, not a latent bug to change to a KV-specific type.
ledger.why: If it were wrong, the KV helper's template argument would be ambiguous and the extraction could silently change behavior.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified: every reinterpret_cast in kv_client_c_wrapper.cpp (lines 63, 91, 137, 186, 226, 278, 313, ...) casts to std::shared_ptr<ObjectClientImpl> *; the KV handle is uniformly ObjectClientImpl-backed. Instantiate the KV helper with ObjectClientImpl.

blast_radius.why: Preserving the existing cast type keeps behavior identical to today.

reversibility.why: The template argument is a one-token change if a separate issue re-types the KV handle.

ledger.revisit_if: A separate issue re-types the KV handle away from ObjectClientImpl.

ledger.what: util.h already transitively provides everything the template body needs (std::shared_ptr, std::string, datasystem::SensitiveValue, datasystem::Status, datasystem::K_OK, StatusC, ToStatusC), so no new #include is required in util.h, and each client type is complete at its instantiation site.
ledger.why: A template body is only type-checked at instantiation; the header must still name the utility types it references directly, and each .cpp must see the complete client type.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified: util.h includes object_client_impl.h, sensitive_value.h, status.h, status_definition.h and declares ToStatusC, and already uses std::string/std::shared_ptr in existing declarations; the three .cpp files each include their client impl / stream_client.h header. Plan Task 1 Step 2 adds a syntax-only fallback to add <memory>/<string> if the build complains.

blast_radius.why: At worst a missing-include compile error surfaces immediately at build; no runtime impact.

reversibility.why: Add the missing #include if the build reports it.

ledger.revisit_if: The build reports an incomplete-type or unknown-symbol error in any of the three translation units.

ledger.what: Name the helper UpdateAkSkImpl (template) to disambiguate it from the member function UpdateAkSk it calls, rather than reusing the bare name UpdateAkSk in the style of ConnectWorker/FreeClient.
ledger.why: Readability: (*client)->UpdateAkSk(...) inside a same-named free function is confusing; the sibling helpers happen to have non-colliding names.
ledger.what_i_did: Chose UpdateAkSkImpl as the template name.

blast_radius.why: Internal name only; no exported symbol or ABI is affected.

reversibility.why: A rename is a mechanical find/replace across four files.

ledger.revisit_if: A reviewer prefers matching the plain ConnectWorker/FreeClient naming convention.

ledger.what: No new isolated unit test is added for the three wrappers; verification relies on a clean build plus the existing AK/SK auth ST tests.
ledger.why: The C wrappers require a live client handle / worker; existing coverage is ST-level AK/SK auth tests, and the repo has no unit harness that constructs these handles in isolation. Adding one exceeds this dedup's surgical scope.
ledger.what_i_did: Relied on existing AK/SK ST tests (kv_client_tenant_akskauth_test, sc_client_aksk_auth_test) plus compile verification; documented the gap in the plan's Task 5.

blast_radius.why: A pure behavior-preserving extraction keeps regression risk low, but with no targeted test a subtle instantiation error would only be caught by the broader ST suite.

reversibility.why: A dedicated test can be added later without reworking the helper.

ledger.revisit_if: The reviewer requires a dedicated regression test, or a unit harness for the C wrappers already exists and was missed.
